title: oxalis fluminensis f. subglabrata
intro: Oxalis fluminensis f. subglabrata, also known as the Brazilian wood sorrel, is a plant species within the oxalis genus, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Oxalis fluminensis f. subglabrata include its potential to help with digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and treat certain skin conditions. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
